successCriteria.description: activeWork entries not in paused/completed/failed/shipping states whose lastActivity (or createdAt as fallback) is older than stalenessThresholdHours are flagged as stale
Stale WorkProgressCard items show a warning icon and a muted/dimmed CSS class in the sidebar Active Work section
Stale cards display last activity in human-readable relative form (e.g. 'last activity 3h ago')
A toast notification of type 'warning' fires once per newly-detected stale item (deduped; not on every poll tick)
Three quick actions — Pause, Reset to pending, Remove — are available on issue cards and write to state.json via the update_work_status IPC command
A 'Staleness Threshold' setting (hours, default 24) is persisted in settingsStore workflow settings and wired to a number input in SettingsPage
Stale detection runs at app startup and on a 5-minute interval in the frontend

phases.title: Rust update_work_status IPC command
phases.content:
Add a new Tauri IPC command `update_work_status` in `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs` that reads state.json, applies a mutation, and atomic-writes it back. Register it in `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`.

Design:
1. Add a `WorkAction` enum at the top of commands.rs: derive `serde::Deserialize` and annotate with `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` so the frontend can pass plain strings. Variants: `Pause`, `Reset`, `Remove`.
2. Update the state import to include `WorkContext` and `WorkStatus`: `use crate::state::{TikiPlan, TikiRelease, TikiState, WorkContext, WorkStatus};`.
3. Implement the command with signature: `pub fn update_work_status(work_id: String, action: WorkAction, tiki_path: Option<String>) -> Result<(), String>`. Body:
   - Resolve path via the existing `resolve_tiki_path` helper.
   - `let state_file = path.join("state.json");`
   - Read state: `let mut state = fs_utils::read_json_resilient::<TikiState>(&state_file)?.ok_or_else(|| "state.json not found".to_string())?;`
   - Match on `action`: for `Pause` and `Reset`, call `state.active_work.get_mut(&work_id).ok_or("work item not found")`, then match `WorkContext::Issue(ref mut ctx)` and mutate `ctx.status = WorkStatus::Paused` (or `WorkStatus::Pending` + `ctx.phase = None` for Reset). Return an error for Release variants. For `Remove`, call `state.active_work.remove(&work_id);` (no error on missing key).
   - Update `ctx.last_activity = Some(chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339());` for Pause/Reset.
   - Serialize: `let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&state).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;`
   - Write: `fs_utils::atomic_write(&state_file, &content)?;`
4. Add `#[tauri::command]` attribute above the function.
5. In `lib.rs`, add `commands::update_work_status` to the `tauri::generate_handler![...]` list.

The file watcher emits `stateChanged` when state.json is overwritten, so the frontend reloads automatically.
verification: cargo check in apps/desktop/src-tauri passes with no errors
WorkAction enum exists with Pause/Reset/Remove variants and #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
update_work_status is registered in the tauri::generate_handler! list in lib.rs
pnpm build from repo root passes

phases.title: Settings stalenessThresholdHours field and SettingsPage UI
phases.content:
Add a `stalenessThresholdHours` field to workflow settings and wire a number input in SettingsPage. Design: 1. In `apps/desktop/src/stores/settingsStore.ts`: - Add `stalenessThresholdHours: number` to the `WorkflowSettings` interface after `defaultPlanningType`. - Add `stalenessThresholdHours: 24` to `DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_SETTINGS`. - Because `initialState` spreads `DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_SETTINGS` and zustand/persist merges on hydration, existing persisted state without this key will automatically receive the default value — no migration function required. 2. In `apps/desktop/src/components/settings/SettingsPage.tsx`: - Inside the Workflow `
` block, after the last existing `settings-row` (defaultPlanningType select), add a new `settings-row` with label 'Staleness Threshold' and a number input: ` updateWorkflow({ stalenessThresholdHours: Number(e.target.value) })} min={1} max={720} step={1} />`. - Add a hint paragraph below the input: `

Work items with no activity for this many hours are flagged as stale.

`. - No changes to `isWorkflowDefault` are needed because JSON.stringify includes the new field on both sides.
verification: WorkflowSettings interface includes stalenessThresholdHours: number
DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_SETTINGS.stalenessThresholdHours is 24
SettingsPage renders a number input for Staleness Threshold in the Workflow section
pnpm build from repo root passes

phases.title: useStaleWorkDetection hook, App.tsx wiring, and StateSection prop threading
phases.content:
Create the stale detection hook and thread its output from App.tsx down to WorkProgressCard via StateSection. This phase also sets up the 5-minute polling interval and fires deduped toast notifications.

Design:
1. Create `apps/desktop/src/hooks/useStaleWorkDetection.ts`.
   - Imports: `useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef` from react; `WorkContext` from `../components/work/WorkCard`; `useToastStore` from `../stores/toastStore`.
   - Free helper `computeStaleFlags(activeWork: Record<string, WorkContext>, thresholdHours: number): Record<string, boolean>`: iterate entries, skip non-issue types and entries whose `status` is in `['paused', 'completed', 'failed', 'shipping']`. For the rest, compute `elapsedMs = Date.now() - new Date(work.lastActivity ?? work.createdAt).getTime()` and set flag to `elapsedMs > thresholdHours * 3_600_000`.
   - Hook signature: `export function useStaleWorkDetection(activeWork: Record<string, WorkContext>, thresholdHours: number): Record<string, boolean>`.
   - Internal state: `const [staleFlags, setStaleFlags] = useState<Record<string, boolean>>(() => computeStaleFlags(activeWork, thresholdHours));`.
   - `notifiedRef = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set())` — tracks workIds that have already fired a toast.
   - `useEffect` (deps `[activeWork, thresholdHours]`): define `runDetection` that calls `computeStaleFlags`, updates `staleFlags`, then for each key: if newly stale and not in `notifiedRef`, call `useToastStore.getState().addToast(msg, 'warning', 8000)` where `msg = \`Issue #${work.issue.number} has been executing for ${Math.floor(elapsedHours)}h with no activity\`` and add to `notifiedRef`; if no longer stale, delete from `notifiedRef`. Call `runDetection()` immediately, then `const id = setInterval(runDetection, 300_000)`. Return `() => clearInterval(id)`.
2. Export from `apps/desktop/src/hooks/index.ts`: add `export { useStaleWorkDetection } from './useStaleWorkDetection';`. If hooks/index.ts does not exist, the hook can be imported directly via its path.
3. In `apps/desktop/src/App.tsx`:
   - Import `useStaleWorkDetection`.
   - Add a `stalenessThresholdHours` selector: `const stalenessThresholdHours = useSettingsStore((s) => s.workflow.stalenessThresholdHours);`.
   - Call the hook inside `App`: `const staleFlags = useStaleWorkDetection(state?.activeWork ?? {}, stalenessThresholdHours);`.
   - Update the `StateSection` JSX: `<StateSection activeWork={state.activeWork} staleFlags={staleFlags} />`.
4. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/StateSection.tsx`:
   - Add `staleFlags?: Record<string, boolean>` to `StateSectionProps`.
   - In the `workEntries.map`, pass `isStale={staleFlags?.[key] ?? false}` and `workId={key}` to `WorkProgressCard`.
5. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/WorkProgressCard.tsx`:
   - Add `workId: string` and `isStale: boolean` to `WorkProgressCardProps`. The component body does not yet use them — that is phases 4 and 5. TypeScript requires the props to be declared so the build passes.
verification: useStaleWorkDetection.ts exists in apps/desktop/src/hooks/
Hook returns Record<string, boolean> and schedules a 300000ms interval via setInterval
Toast fires via useToastStore.getState().addToast with type 'warning' for each newly-stale item, deduped via notifiedRef
App.tsx passes staleFlags to StateSection
StateSection passes isStale and workId to WorkProgressCard
WorkProgressCard declares workId: string and isStale: boolean in its props interface
pnpm build from repo root passes

phases.title: Stale visual treatment on WorkProgressCard
phases.content:
Use the `isStale` prop (wired in phase 3) to apply a dimmed CSS modifier, a warning icon, and a 'last activity Xh ago' relative timestamp to stale WorkProgressCards. Design: 1. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/WorkProgressCard.tsx`: - In the card root div, add the stale class: change the className expression from `` `work-progress-card status-${work.status}` `` to `` `work-progress-card status-${work.status}${isStale ? ' stale' : ''}` ``. - Inside `.work-progress-header`, add a warning icon between the type label and the status badge: `{isStale && ⚠}`. - Compute the relative timestamp near the top of the component body (after existing const declarations): `const activityTimestamp = work.type === 'issue' ? (work.lastActivity ?? work.createdAt) : work.createdAt;` and `const staleHours = Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(activityTimestamp).getTime()) / 3_600_000);`. - Immediately after the `.work-progress-header` div, add: `{isStale &&
last activity {staleHours}h ago
}`. 2. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/WorkProgressCard.css` (or whichever CSS file the card uses — check imports in WorkProgressCard.tsx), append: - `.work-progress-card.stale { opacity: 0.65; }` - `.work-progress-stale-icon { color: #f59e0b; font-size: 12px; margin: 0 4px; }` - `.work-progress-stale-timestamp { font-size: 10px; color: #f59e0b; margin-bottom: 6px; opacity: 0.85; }`
verification: A stale card renders with CSS class 'stale' and visually dimmed at 65% opacity
The warning icon appears in the card header for stale cards only
The 'last activity Xh ago' line appears below the header for stale cards
Non-stale cards are visually unchanged
pnpm build from repo root passes

phases.title: Quick action buttons: Pause, Reset, Remove
phases.content:
Add hover-revealed Pause, Reset, and Remove buttons to WorkProgressCard. Buttons invoke the `update_work_status` IPC command from phase 1. Remove shows an inline confirmation before acting.

Design:
1. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/WorkProgressCard.tsx`:
   - Add local state: `const [showConfirmRemove, setShowConfirmRemove] = useState(false);`.
   - Import `invoke` from `@tauri-apps/api/core` if not already imported.
   - Read the active project for tikiPath: get `activeProject` from `useProjectsStore` if not already in scope.
   - Add a handler: `async function handleAction(action: 'pause' | 'reset' | 'remove') { const tikiPath = activeProject?.path ? \`${activeProject.path}/.tiki\` : undefined; try { await invoke('update_work_status', { workId, action, tikiPath }); } catch (e) { console.error('update_work_status failed:', e); } }`.
   - Render actions row only for issue-type cards (`isIssue && !showConfirmRemove`): a `<div className="work-progress-actions">` containing three `<button>` elements — 'Pause' (calls `handleAction('pause')`), 'Reset' (calls `handleAction('reset')`), 'Remove' (calls `setShowConfirmRemove(true)`). Give the Remove button an additional `work-action-btn--danger` class.
   - When `showConfirmRemove` is true, render `<div className="work-progress-confirm-remove">` with text 'Remove this item?' and two buttons: 'Confirm' (calls `handleAction('remove')` then `setShowConfirmRemove(false)`) and 'Cancel' (calls `setShowConfirmRemove(false)`).
   - Place the actions/confirm block at the very end of the card JSX.
   - After a successful mutation, the file watcher fires a `stateChanged` event and App.tsx reloads state from disk — the card updates or disappears automatically.
2. In `apps/desktop/src/components/sidebar/WorkProgressCard.css`, append:
   - `.work-progress-actions { display: none; gap: 4px; margin-top: 8px; justify-content: flex-end; }`
   - `.work-progress-card:hover .work-progress-actions { display: flex; }`
   - `.work-action-btn { font-size: 10px; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 3px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: var(--text-secondary, #858585); cursor: pointer; }`
   - `.work-action-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); color: var(--text-primary, #cccccc); }`
   - `.work-action-btn--danger:hover { background: rgba(239,68,68,0.2); color: #f87171; border-color: rgba(239,68,68,0.3); }`
   - `.work-progress-confirm-remove { margin-top: 8px; font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-secondary, #858585); display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; justify-content: flex-end; }`
   - `.work-progress-confirm-remove button { font-size: 10px; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 3px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: var(--text-secondary, #858585); cursor: pointer; }`
verification: Hovering over an issue-type WorkProgressCard reveals Pause, Reset, and Remove buttons
Clicking Remove shows inline confirmation; Confirm calls invoke('update_work_status', { workId, action: 'remove', tikiPath })
Clicking Pause calls invoke with action 'pause'; the card status updates after the watcher fires stateChanged
Clicking Reset calls invoke with action 'reset'; the card reverts to pending status
Release-type cards do not show action buttons (isIssue guard)
pnpm build from repo root passes
